briefdescription.para: The data type (and object index) of an identity record.
The static initializer for struct co_id.
The maximum number of CANopen networks.
The maximum number of nodes in a CANopen network.
A bit rate of 1 Mbit/s.
A bit rate of 800 kbit/s.
A bit rate of 500 kbit/s.
A bit rate of 250 kbit/s.
A bit rate of 125 kbit/s.
A bit rate of 50 kbit/s.
A bit rate of 20 kbit/s.
A bit rate of 10 kbit/s.
Automatic bit rate detection.

memberdef.name: LELY_CO_DEFINE_TYPE
memberdef.initializer: co_##b##_t co_dev_get_val_##c(const co_dev_t *dev, \ co_unsigned16_t idx, co_unsigned8_t subidx); \ size_t co_dev_set_val_##c(co_dev_t *dev, co_unsigned16_t idx, \ co_unsigned8_t subidx, co_##b##_t c);

briefdescription.para: The type of a CANopen Transmit-PDO event indication function, invoked by co_dev_tpdo_event() when an event is indicated for (a sub-object mapped into) an acyclic or event-driven PDO.

parameterdescription.para: the PDO number (in the range [1..512]).
a pointer to user-specified data.

briefdescription.para: Initializes a co_dev_t instance.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer ot an uninitialized co_dev_t instance.
the node-ID of the device (in the range [1..127, 255]). If id is 255, the device is unconfigured.

simplesect.para: dev, or NULL on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Finalizes a co_dev_t instance.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a valid co_dev_t instance.

briefdescription.para: Returns the network-ID of a CANopen device.
Sets the network-ID of a CANopen device.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Returns the node-ID of a CANopen device.
Sets the node-ID of a CANopen device.

detaileddescription.para: This function will also update any sub-object values of the form $NODEID { "+" number }.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Retrieves a list of object indices in the object dictionary of a CANopen device.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen device.
the maximum number of object indices to return.
an array of at least maxidx indices (can be NULL). On success, *idx contains the object indices.

simplesect.para: the total number of object indices in the object dictionary (which may be different from maxidx).

briefdescription.para: Inserts an object into the object dictionary of a CANopen device.

detaileddescription.para: This function fails if the object is already part of the object dictionary of another device, or if another object with the same index already exists.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen device.
a pointer to the object to be inserted.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error.
co_dev_remove_obj(), co_dev_find_obj()

briefdescription.para: Removes an object from the object dictionary a CANopen device.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen device.
a pointer to the object to be removed.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error.

briefdescription.para: Finds an object in the object dictionary of a CANopen device.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen device.
the object index.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the object if found, or NULL if not.

briefdescription.para: Finds a sub-object in the object dictionary of a CANopen device.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen device.
the object index.
the object sub-index.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the sub-object if found, or NULL if not.

briefdescription.para: Finds the first object (with the lowest index) in the object dictionary of a CANopen device.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the first object, or NULL if the object dictionary is empty.
co_dev_last_obj(), co_obj_next()

briefdescription.para: Finds the last object (with the highest index) in the object dictionary of a CANopen device.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the last object, or NULL if the object dictionary is empty.
co_dev_first_obj(), co_obj_prev()

briefdescription.para: Returns the vendor ID of a CANopen device.
Sets the vendor ID of a CANopen device.
Returns the product code of a CANopen device.
Sets the product code of a CANopen device.
Returns the revision number of a CANopen device.
Sets the revision number of a CANopen device.
Returns the supported bit rates of a CANopen device (any combination of CO_BAUD_1000, CO_BAUD_800, CO_BAUD_500, CO_BAUD_250, CO_BAUD_125, CO_BAUD_50, CO_BAUD_20, CO_BAUD_10 and CO_BAUD_AUTO).
Sets the supported bit rates of a CANopen device.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen device.
the supported bit rates (any combination of CO_BAUD_1000, CO_BAUD_800, CO_BAUD_500, CO_BAUD_250, CO_BAUD_125, CO_BAUD_50, CO_BAUD_20, CO_BAUD_10 and CO_BAUD_AUTO).

briefdescription.para: Returns the (pending) baudrate of a CANopen device (in kbit/s).
Sets the (pending) baudrate of a CANopen device.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen device.
the baudrate (in kbit/s).

briefdescription.para: Returns true if LSS is supported and false if not.
Sets the LSS support flag.
Returns the data types supported by a CANopen device for mapping dummy entries in PDOs (one bit for each of the basic types).
Sets the data types supported by a CANopen device for mapping dummy entries in PDOs.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen device.
the data types supported for mapping dummy entries in PDOs (one bit for each of the basic types).

briefdescription.para: Returns a pointer to the current value of a CANopen sub-object.

detaileddescription.para: In the case of strings or domains, this is the address of a pointer to the first byte in the array.

simplesect.para: co_dev_set_val(), co_sub_get_val()

memberdef.argsstring: (co_dev_t *dev, co_unsigned16_t idx, co_unsigned8_t subidx, const void *ptr, size_t n)
memberdef.name: co_dev_set_val

briefdescription.para: Sets the current value of a CANopen sub-object.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen device.
the object index.
the object sub-index.
a pointer to the bytes to be copied. In case of strings or domains, ptr MUST point to the first byte in the array.
the number of bytes at ptr. In case of strings, n SHOULD exclude the terminating null byte(s).

simplesect.para: the number of bytes copied (i.e., n), or 0 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().
co_dev_get_val(), co_sub_set_val()

memberdef.argsstring: (co_dev_t *dev, co_unsigned16_t *pidx, co_unsigned8_t *psubidx, const uint_least8_t *begin, const uint_least8_t *end)
memberdef.name: co_dev_read_sub

briefdescription.para: Reads a value from a memory buffer, in the concise DCF format, and stores it in a sub-object in the object dictionary of a CANopen device.

detaileddescription.para: If the sub-object does not exist, the value is discarded.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen device.
the address at which to store the object index (can be NULL).
the address at which to store the object sub-index (can be NULL).
a pointer to the start of the buffer.
a pointer to one past the last byte in the buffer.

simplesect.para: the number of bytes read on success (at least 7), or 0 on error.

memberdef.argsstring: (const co_dev_t *dev, co_unsigned16_t idx, co_unsigned8_t subidx, uint_least8_t *begin, uint_least8_t *end)
memberdef.name: co_dev_write_sub

briefdescription.para: Loads the value of a sub-object from the object dictionary of a CANopen device, and writes it to a memory buffer, in the concise DCF format.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen device.
the object index.
the object sub-index.
a pointer to the start of the buffer. If begin is NULL, nothing is written.
a pointer to one past the last byte in the buffer. If end is not NULL, and the buffer is too small (i.e., end - begin is less than the return value), nothing is written.

simplesect.para: the number of bytes that would have been written had the buffer been sufficiently large, or 0 on error.

memberdef.argsstring: (co_dev_t *dev, co_unsigned16_t *pmin, co_unsigned16_t *pmax, const uint_least8_t *begin, const uint_least8_t *end)
memberdef.name: co_dev_read_dcf

briefdescription.para: Reads the values of a range of objects from a memory buffer, in the concise DCF format, and stores them in the object dictionary of a CANopen device.

detaileddescription.para: If an object does not exist, the value is discarded.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen device.
the address at which to store the minimum object index (can be NULL).
the address at which to store the maximum object index (can be NULL).
a pointer to the start of the buffer.
a pointer to one past the last byte in the buffer.

simplesect.para: the number of bytes read on success (at least 4), or 0 on error.

briefdescription.para: Reads the values of a range of objects from a file, in the concise DCF format, and stores them in the object dictionary of a CANopen device.

detaileddescription.para: If an object does not exist, the value is discarded.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen device.
the address at which to store the minimum object index (can be NULL).
the address at which to store the maximum object index (can be NULL).
a pointer to the name of the file.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

memberdef.argsstring: (const co_dev_t *dev, co_unsigned16_t min, co_unsigned16_t max, uint_least8_t *begin, uint_least8_t *end)
memberdef.name: co_dev_write_dcf

briefdescription.para: Loads the values of a range of objects in the object dictionary of a CANopen device, and writes them to a memory buffer, in the concise DCF format.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen device.
the minimum object index.
the maximum object index.
a pointer to the start of the buffer. If begin is NULL, nothing is written.
a pointer to one past the last byte in the buffer.

simplesect.para: the number of bytes that would have been written had the buffer been sufficiently large, or 0 on error.

memberdef.argsstring: (const co_dev_t *dev, co_unsigned16_t min, co_unsigned16_t max, const char *filename)
memberdef.name: co_dev_write_dcf_file

briefdescription.para: Loads the values of a range of objects in the object dictionary of a CANopen device, and writes them to a file, in the concise DCF format.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen device.
the minimum object index.
the maximum object index.
a pointer to the name of the file.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the indication function invoked by co_dev_tpdo_event() when an event is indicated for (a sub-object mapped into) an acyclic or event-driven Transmit-PDO.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen device.
the address at which to store a pointer to the indication function (can be NULL).
the address at which to store a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL).

briefdescription.para: Sets the indication function invoked by co_dev_tpdo_event() when an event is indicated for (a sub-object mapped into) an acyclic or event-driven Transmit-PDO.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen device.
a pointer to the function to be invoked.
a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL). data is passed as the last parameter to ind.

briefdescription.para: Checks if the specified sub-object in the object dictionary of a CANopen device can be mapped into a PDO and, if so, issues an indication for every valid, acylic or event-driven Transmit-PDO into which the sub-object is mapped by invoking the user-defined callback function set with co_dev_set_tpdo_event_ind().

detaileddescription.para: At most one event is indicated for every matching TPDO.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen device.
the object index.
the object sub-index.

briefdescription.para: This header file is part of the CANopen library; it contains the device description declarations.

detaileddescription.para:
